chunks.text: Page 1 of 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P. No. D- 1453 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case For hearing of main case 15-12-2022 Mr. Faisal Shams, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Nisar Ahmed G. Abro, D.A.G. Muhammad Irfan-ul-Haq Divisional Superintendent Pakistan Railways, Sukkur is in attendance. ***** Mr. Ghulam Abbas Akhtar, Advocate files power on behalf of respondents No.3&5-Pakitan Railways so also statement and objections on behalf of said respondents, which are taken on record and copies of the statement and objections are supplied to learned counsel for the petitioners. It appears from perusal of the statement of respondent No.5 that the petitioner No.1 retired as STE of Pakistan Railways on 02.08.2021 on voluntarily basis; he is receiving monthly pension regularly and has already received GP Fund. However, an amount of Rs. 2,546,434/- towards Commutation and Rs. 792,442/- towards Encashment are to be paid to the petitioner No.1, while petitioner No.2 retired as STE on 31.12.2021 on voluntarily basis; he is also receiving monthly pension regularly and has received GP Fund. However, amount of Rs. 2,702,630/- towards Commutation and Rs. 918,288/- towards Encashment are to be paid to the petitioner No.2 Since the amount of Commutation and Encashment are admitted by the respondents-Pakistan Railways, instant petition is disposed of by directing the Financial Advisor / Chief Accounts Officer, Pakistan Railways Headquarter office, Lahore and respondents No. 3 to 5 within 30 days hereof. Instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE
